question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary's readiness check does not verify the pinger's network configuration (cfg.network), allowing a pinger configured with network='mainnet' instead of 'testnet' to pass readiness checks and submit fee-bearing CryptoTransfer transactions on the mainnet, where fees have real monetary value outside the design-intended test parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary does not verify the pinger's cfg.toAccountID before returning os.Exit(0), allowing a pinger configured with a malicious destination account to pass readiness checks and submit transactions that route fees to an attacker-controlled account outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary's liveness check does not verify the pinger's cfg.operatorKey validity, allowing a pinger with a compromised operator key to pass liveness checks and submit transactions that are signed with an unauthorized key, potentially modifying the effective fee payer outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary does not check whether the pinger's cfg.mirrorNodeClientTimeout is within design-specified bounds, allowing a pinger with mirrorNodeClientTimeout=0 to pass health checks while buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() times out immediately, causing the pinger to fall back to a default network configuration with different fee structures?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary's 'ready' mode does not check whether the pinger's cfg.mirrorRest URL is valid when network='other', allowing a pinger configured with a malicious mirror REST endpoint to pass readiness checks and build a network topology from attacker-controlled node data, routing transactions to nodes with different fee structures?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the healthcheck binary does not implement any mechanism to detect if the pinger's amountTinybar has changed between pod restarts, allowing an attacker to modify HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR via a ConfigMap or Secret update and have the new value take effect on the next pod restart triggered by a manipulated liveness probe failure?
